name: szilard_distinguishability_admission_bridge
classification_note: Canonical bridge row: maps the classical Szilard-Landauer bookkeeping (mutual information / free-energy / erasure cost) onto the constraint-admissibility frame (distinguishability / admission-gate / F01 floor). The nonclassical claim is that free-erasure-after-admission is not 'expensive' but 'inadmissible' -- certified as UNSAT by z3.

pytorch.reason: 2-qubit bookkeeping row uses numpy; no gradient surface needed for the bridge

pyg.reason: no graph carrier in this bridge row

z3.reason: Encodes the Szilard<->admissibility bridge axioms (F01 = T*D, demon dF<=E, admission-gated floor) and certifies the nonclassical forbidden region (admitted AND Ev < F01) is UNSAT; plus a counterfactual-UNSAT and an independence-SAT row.

cvc5.reason: z3 already covers the Real-arithmetic admission encoding

sympy.reason: closed-form one-bit values are numeric-exact; no symbolic step needed

clifford.reason: no geometric-algebra carrier in this bridge row

geomstats.reason: no manifold carrier in this bridge row

e3nn.reason: no equivariant representation in this bridge row

z3_bridge_proof.note: z3 is load-bearing: the bridge's nonclassical claim (free-erasure of an admitted record is structurally inadmissible, not merely expensive) is certified by UNSAT of (admitted AND Ev < F01). The independence SAT row gates the forbidden region to the admission branch, preventing a vacuous global constraint.

summary.scope_note: Bridge row only: identifies Szilard quantities with admission-gate variables on a two-qubit carrier. No engine-runtime, reservoir, or universal-demon claim.
